DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-19-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant The Regents of the University of California's demurrer to Plaintiff Donald S. Hollingsworth's complaint is overruled.

Emergency Rule of Court 9 adopted by the Judicial Council provides that "the statutes of limitations and repose for civil causes of action that exceed 180 days are tolled from April 6, 2020, until October 1, 2020." (Cal. Rules of Court, App. I, Rule 9.) The one-year statute of limitations for medical malpractice actions in Code of Civil Procedure section 340.5 obviously exceeds 180 days. Tolling of statutes of limitations will "suspend or extend a statute of limitations . . . ." (McDonald v. Antelope Valley Community College Dist. (2008) 45 Cal.4th 88, 99.) Accordingly, Defendant does not show that Plaintiff's July 26, 2021, complaint was untimely.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
